mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative repricing of equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% during synchronized downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding corporate output capacity and margin improvement, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant relationship with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation breaches 205.0, it forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 205.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with negative returns occurring in more than 55% of matched periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signal reduced construction activity and lower household wealth effects, which immediately depresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive power disappears when FEDFUNDS is stable.
